metadata.notes: agy has an internal approval_request path, but a tool-gate permission prompt is an in-progress state never persisted to the transcript (all lines status:DONE) and the adapter has no PermissionPending signal. daemon=incapable → frozen. Matches codex/gemini-cli 2.19 (incapable).

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready (n/a — frozen)

Route: frozen (the permission-prompt `waiting` episode is not observable for antigravity).

## What the scenario pins

`ready → working → waiting`; after the prompt resolves `waiting → working → ready`; a delayed post-tool hook arriving after `→ ready` does NOT bounce state. Final state: `waiting`; the `waiting` episode is user-visible.

## agent_supports: yes

The agy binary has a tool-approval path (binary strings: `approval_request`), and tools like run_command can be gated before execution, so the agent CAN open a permission prompt.

## daemon_capability: incapable

The canonical claudecode cell validates the rule-0 PermissionPending→waiting gate, which is hook-driven. Antigravity has NO such hook and NO PermissionPending input: the adapter is transcript-first and the parser has no waiting / permission emission path. And the prompt itself is never persisted: every line in BOTH transcript.jsonl and transcript_full.jsonl is status:"DONE" (verified across all real conversations) — a tool awaiting approval is an in-progress (non-DONE) state appended only once resolved. No approval_request / waiting marker appears in any recorded transcript. No trace exists → the 3-state model cannot represent the gate for this agent. This matches the codex and gemini-cli 2.19 verdicts (both daemon=incapable, frozen).

## driver_capability: n/a

Moot under a frozen route.
caveats: Re-assess if Antigravity persists a permission-pending / approval-pending step to the transcript, or if the adapter gains a permission signal sourced outside the transcript (hook or SQLite store).

sources.note: no PermissionPending / waiting emission path; the parser only knows working/ready
100% status:"DONE"; no approval_request marker — the prompt is never persisted
binary strings: approval_request exists internally, but it never reaches the tailed transcript
sibling codex & gemini-cli 2.19 cells are both daemon=incapable/frozen for the same transcript-only-no-permission-signal reason
